programmes.show_description: Victoria is in desperate need of a tattoo cover-up so she can train as a teacher. Abigail gets inked at the age of 75 and Hollie loses her cheeky chicken tattoo.
There's eraser eating, camera wrecking and caravan crushing in the final episode of the series. Which of our intrepid comics will emerge triumphant?
Jimmy Carr hosts as Sean Lock and Joe Wilkinson take on Jon Richardson and Jayde Adams. David Baddiel joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. With maths whizz Rachel Riley.
Teleshopping
Teleshopping
As CEO, Wan Kim has doubled Smoothie King's number of stores and tripled sales. He goes undercover to find out what his changes have meant for his employees and customers.
Colie Edison created every element of the bowling giant Bowlero. Now she goes undercover to find out how the brand is working for staff and customers.
In East Anglia, there's collapsing food and cheeky shortcuts, as competitive Kim and Martin, landscape gardeners Debbie and Richard, and gadget gurus Jamie and Mark compete.
In Plymouth, there's a Chris crisis as half of the rival diners share the same name! Will a locally sourced supper, celebrity fancy dress or a nautical theme win the week?
...Selling: Eric and Misty's DIY projects cause Jonathan some housing havoc. Before Drew can find their next place, the couple must prioritise agreeing on their priorities.
The Big Ibizan Zoo One: Sonny is a party fanatic. How will his bride Jazmin greet his plans for a zoo-themed Ibizan beach party wedding with everyone covered in animal-print body paint?
The One in the Sky: The sky's the limit for adrenaline junkie Jai who plans to have a wedding in the sky, even though his bride-to-be Leanne is terrified of heights.
Linda L. Chadwick, CEO of Rita's Italian Ice & Frozen Custard, goes undercover to find out how her new initiatives are working and to help franchisees become more profitable.
Andrew Simon, CEO of Fremont Street Experience, who started at the Las Vegas attraction at the start of the pandemic, goes undercover to discover and address issues.
Old Neighborhood: Gordon visits Arvada in Colorado, where out-of-control hoarder Randy and his wife Alexa have allowed their restaurant Old Neighborhood to fall into disrepair.
Kati Allo: Gordon's in New York, where the stress of running a Greek restaurant has driven Manny and Christine to the brink of divorce, with their daughter stuck in the middle.
...and Selling: Brand new series: It's convention vs contemporary when Krystal and Cam can't agree on their new home. The twins help with compromises and costly constructions.
She Loves Me Not: Captain Glenn is proud of the crew and their pride charter, but he also has bad news for them. And Gary hears about Mads' three-way kiss.
David Baddiel, Ed Gamble, Jo Brand, Katy Wix and Rose Matafeo make very dramatic entrances, as Greg Davies returns, dragging Little Alex Horne behind him.
Jimmy Carr hosts as Jon Richardson and James Acaster take on Lee Mack and Sarah Millican, with John Cooper Clarke in Dictionary Corner.
Jimmy Carr hosts the panel/quiz show mash-up. Jon Richardson and Chris Addison go up against Joe Wilkinson and Katherine Ryan, with David O'Doherty in Dictionary Corner.
The Piano: Dad has bought a piano, and the world's most sarcastic piano tuner comes round to tune it. Meanwhile, the family spot Aunty Val having an affair. What should they do?
The Big Day: It's Grandma's wedding day, and she's getting married to the horrible Mr Morris. The family are distraught, but will Grandma really say 'I do'?
Will needs Lou's help covering a famous face in an intimate place. Jay assists glamour model Gigi, who has the initials of not one, but two exes on her breast.
David Baddiel, Ed Gamble, Jo Brand, Katy Wix and Rose Matafeo make very dramatic entrances, as Greg Davies returns, dragging Little Alex Horne behind him.
Jimmy Carr hosts as Jon Richardson and James Acaster take on Lee Mack and Sarah Millican, with John Cooper Clarke in Dictionary Corner.
Teleshopping
Teleshopping
Mayor Adrian Perkins of Shreveport, Louisiana, is the city's second youngest mayor and in charge of 3000 employees. He goes undercover, hoping to change the city's fortunes.
Omar Soliman and Nick Friedman co-founded College HUNKS Hauling Junk & Moving over 15 years ago. They go undercover to see if their company is strong enough to withstand growth.
In Aberdeen, there's leg wrestling, a shocking revelation and a guest appearance from a goat, as Nicola and Russell, Claire and Gerry, and Megan and Sean compete.
Three rival couples compete in Cambridge, where stable owner Alice's feisty boyfriend Ollie reveals that he helped to hunt and butcher the deer for their main course...
...Selling: Newlyweds Mayra and Chris vow to find a home for all their blended family to enjoy. Can Drew find a new place that ticks all the boxes?
The Sk8er Boi One: Will Andy's plans for a skater-themed wedding make his bride Bobby's wheels spin? Or will there be broken bones and broken hearts on the big day?
The Obstacle Course One: Chris wants his bride to jump through hoops, and plans to turn his wedding into an obstacle course. But Charlotte dreams of a traditional family wedding.
Mitchell Modell, CEO of Modell's Sporting Goods, America's oldest family-owned company selling athletics apparel and equipment, goes incognito in his own firm.
Kat Cole, the president of Cinnabon Incorporated, which serves sweet treats from over 900 bakeries in 40 countries, goes incognito among her own workforce.
Mangia Mangia (Part 1/2): Gordon visits an Italian restaurant with an identity crisis in Colorado, for the first half of a two-part Nightmare. The owner is at breaking point.
Mangia Mangia (Part 2/2): Gordon returns to Colorado to conclude his attempt to help an Italian restaurant. Can he work his magic on a menu that relies on a microwave?
...and Selling: Brand new series: New parents Ela and Noam are looking for a family home. Can the bros provide a stylish makeover and fashion their walk-in wardrobe into a bedroom?
Boat Load of Throuples: Captain Glenn threatens to kick one of the 'throuple' guests off Parsifal during a drunken guest fight. Mads receives a wake-up call.
Greg Davies takes five comics to task on their efforts to complete pointless tasks. Jo Brand and David Baddiel take time out to enjoy a sandwich.
Jimmy Carr hosts as Jon Richardson and Joe Lycett take on Sean Lock and Michelle Wolf. Adam Riches joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. With Rachel Riley.
Jimmy Carr hosts as Jon Richardson and Jonathan Ross take on Lee Mack and Victoria Coren Mitchell. Jessie Cave joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. With Rachel Riley.
The Two Tonys: Dad accidentally invites someone who he used to hate round for dinner: an annoying man who talks with his eyes closed. How will Dad get rid of him?
The Carpet Cleaner: It's Dad's annual hinge oiling evening but when he spills oil on Mum's beloved carpet, it's a race against time to clean it up before she finds out.
